chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD C.P. No.D-416 of 2022 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE For orders on M.A. 7118//2022 For orders on office objection For orders on M.A. 2099/2022 For orders on M.A. 2100/2022 For hearing of main case 26.05.2022 Mr. Karamullah Memon, advocate for petitioner. --- Petitioner seeks regularization of the alleged service in the office of Mukhtiarkar and the sole document that he has relied upon is a letter of one Mukhtiarkar which discloses that he is working in the office of Mukhtiarkar on private basis / need basis and is typing letters of the officers. This could hardly be a ground for the regularization of his service. He was never appointed officially and that too through transparent process required to be adopted while appointing the employees in the revenue department as we expect that a transparent process through advertisement should have been made. Although there was no advertisement but even the appointment of the petitioner is not convincing at all as only letter at page 45 disclosed that he was privately appointed typist in the office of Mukhtiarkar. The petition being misconceived is dismissed. JUDGE JUDGE
